records.chapter: Reward of sweeping the mosque floor
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  It is narrated from Imam Musa Kadhim (a.s.) that the Holy Prophet (s.a.w.s.) said, “If one sweeps the floor of a mosque on a Thursday night and cleans the quantity of dirt equal to the Antimony put in one’s eye then too Allah forgives him.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُوسَی قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ الْعَطَّارُ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَحْمَدَ عَنْ سَهْلِ بْنِ زِیَادٍ عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ بَشَّارٍ عَنْ عُبَیْدِ اللَّهِ الدِّهْقَانِ عَنْ عَبْدِ الْحَمِیدِ عَنْ أَبِی إِبْرَاهِیمَ ع قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص مَنْ کَنَسَ الْمَسْجِدَ یَوْمَ الْخَمِیسِ لَیْلَهَ الْجُمُعَهِ فَأَخْرَجَ مِنْهُ التُّرَابَ قَدْرَ مَا یُذْرَی فِی الْعَیْنِ غُفِرَ لَهُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1.  It is narrated from Imam Musa Kadhim (a.s.) that the Holy Prophet (s.a.w.s.) said,
records.thaqalaynMatn: “If one sweeps the floor of a mosque on a Thursday night and cleans the quantity of dirt equal to the Antimony put in one’s eye then too Allah forgives him.”

records.chapter: Reward of Calling out the Azaan
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan (rah) who said it was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan al-Saffar from Muhammad b. al-Hussain b. Abi al-Khattab from Ja’far b. Basheer al-’Arzami from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “The most elevated people on the Day of Judgment will be those who used to call out the Azaan.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ الصَّفَّارُ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْحُسَیْنِ بْنِ أَبِی الْخَطَّابِ عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ بَشِیرٍ الْعَرْزَمِیِّ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: أَطْوَلُ النَّاسِ أَعْنَاقاً یَوْمَ الْقِیَامَهِ الْمُؤَذِّنُونَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan (rah) who said it was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan al-Saffar from Muhammad b. al-Hussain b. Abi al-Khattab from Ja’far b. Basheer al-’Arzami from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “The most elevated people on the Day of Judgment will be those who used to call out the Azaan.”

records.chapter: Reward of saying Azaan for seven years
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  Imam  Muhammad  Baqir  (a.s.)  says,  “One  who  recites Azaan for seven years with an intention of seeking reward from Allah would appear on the Day of Judgment without any sin to his name.”
records.arabicText: notfound

records.chapter: Reward of reciting Azaan in a Muslim city for a year
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Ahmad b. Idris from Muhammad b. Ali from Mus’ab b. Salam al-Taymi from Sa’d b. Dharif from Abi Ja’far (a.s.) who said: “Paradise becomes incumbent on one who recites Azaan for a year in a city having Muslim population.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی أَحْمَدُ بْنُ إِدْرِیسَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَلِیٍّ عَنْ مُصْعَبِ بْنِ سَلَّامٍ التَّیْمِیِّ عَنْ سَعْدِ بْنِ ظَرِیفٍ عَنْ أَبِی جَعْفَرٍ ع قَالَ: مَنْ أَذَّنَ سَبْعَ سِنِینَ مُحْتَسِباً جَاءَ یَوْمَ الْقِیَامَهِ وَ لَا ذَنْبَ لَهُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Ahmad b. Idris from Muhammad b. Ali from Mus’ab b. Salam al-Taymi from Sa’d b. Dharif from Abi Ja’far (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “Paradise becomes incumbent on one who recites Azaan for a year in a city having Muslim population.”

records.chapter: Reward of repeating Azaan
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Abdillah b. Ja’far al-Himyari from Muhammad b. Isa from al-Hassan b. Mahbub from Jameel b. Salih from al-Harith b. al-Mughira al-Basri from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “One who hears the Muezzin say: Ash-hadu allaa ilaaha illallaahu wa ash-hadu anna muh’ammadar rasoolullaah and testifies it for the sake of seeking reward from Allah by saying Wa anaa ash-hadu allaa ilaaha illallaahu wa anna muh’ammadar rasoolullaahi aktafee bihimaa a’n kulli man abaa wa jah’ada wa uee’nu bihimaa man aqarra wa shahid Almighty Allah forgives him with the forgiveness equal to those who testify in this way and those who don’t.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ الْحِمْیَرِیُّ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عِیسَی عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ جَمِیلِ بْنِ صَالِحٍ عَنِ الْحَارِثِ بْنِ الْمُغِیرَهِ الْبَصْرِیِّ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: مَنْ سَمِعَ الْمُؤَذِّنَ یَقُولُ أَشْهَدُ أَنْ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ وَ أَشْهَدُ أَنَّ مُحَمَّداً رَسُولُ اللَّهِ فَقَالَ مُصَدِّقاً مُحْتَسِباً وَ أَنَا أَشْهَدُ أَنْ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ وَ أَنَّ مُحَمَّداً رَسُولُ اللَّهِ أَکْتَفِی بِهَا عَنْ کُلِّ مَنْ أَبَی وَ جَحَدَ وَ أُعِینُ بِهَا مَنْ أَقَرَّ وَ شَهِدَ إِلَّا غَفَرَ اللَّهُ لَهُ بِعَدَدِ مَنْ أَنْکَرَ وَ جَحَدَ وَ بِعَدَدِ مَنْ أَقَرَّ وَ شَهِدَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Abdillah b. Ja’far al-Himyari from Muhammad b. Isa from al-Hassan b. Mahbub from Jameel b. Salih from al-Harith b. al-Mughira al-Basri from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “One who hears the Muezzin say: Ash-hadu allaa ilaaha illallaahu wa ash-hadu anna muh’ammadar rasoolullaah and testifies it for the sake of seeking reward from Allah by saying Wa anaa ash-hadu allaa ilaaha illallaahu wa anna muh’ammadar rasoolullaahi aktafee bihimaa a’n kulli man abaa wa jah’ada wa uee’nu bihimaa man aqarra wa shahid Almighty Allah forgives him with the forgiveness equal to those who testify in this way and those who don’t.”

records.chapter: Reward of reciting Azaan for ten years seeking reward from Allah
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Musa b. al-Mutawakil who said it was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Yahya from Muhammad b. Ahmad from Muhammad b. Najayh from Muhammad b. Ali from Mus’ab b. Salam al-Taymi from Sa’d b. Dharif from Abi Ja’far (a.s.) who said: “One who recites Azaan for ten years seeking reward from Allah, Allah would forgive him with the forgiveness equal to the number of times he looks at the sky and number of times his voice comes out. Everything, be it dry or wet will testify it. All those who hear this person and pray inside the mosque will also get a part of this reward.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُوسَی بْنِ الْمُتَوَکِّلِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ یَحْیَی عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَحْمَدَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ نَاجِیَهَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَلِیٍّ عَنْ مُصْعَبِ بْنِ سَلَّامٍ التَّیْمِیِّ عَنْ سَعْدِ بْنِ ظَرِیفٍ عَنْ أَبِی جَعْفَرٍ ع قَالَ: مَنْ أَذَّنَ عِشْرِینَ سَنَهً مُحْتَسِباً یَغْفِرُ اللَّهُ لَهُ مَدَّ بَصَرِهِ وَ مَدَّ صَوْتِهِ فِی السَّمَاءِ وَ یُصَدِّقُهُ کُلُّ رَطْبٍ وَ یَابِسٍ سَمِعَهُ وَ لَهُ بِکُلِّ مَنْ یُصَلِّی مَعَهُ فِی مَسْجِدِهِ سَهْمٌ وَ لَهُ مِنْ کُلِّ مَنْ یُصَلِّی بِصَوْتِهِ حَسَنَهٌ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Musa b. al-Mutawakil who said it was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Yahya from Muhammad b. Ahmad from Muhammad b. Najayh from Muhammad b. Ali from Mus’ab b. Salam al-Taymi from Sa’d b. Dharif from Abi Ja’far (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “One who recites Azaan for ten years seeking reward from Allah, Allah would forgive him with the forgiveness equal to the number of times he looks at the sky and number of times his voice comes out. Everything, be it dry or wet will testify it. All those who hear this person and pray inside the mosque will also get a part of this reward.”

records.chapter: Reward of Muezzin between Azaan and Iqamah
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Ali b. Majluwayh who said it was narrated to me by my uncle Muhammad b. Abi al-Qasim from Muhammad b. Ali from Isa b. Abdillah from his father from his grandfather from Ali (a.s.) who said: “No, a time will come when the responsibility of Azaan and Iqamah will be entrusted only to weak and helpless people. Hence the fire of Hell is made unlawful on their weak flesh.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَلِیِّ بْنِ مَاجِیلَوَیْهِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی عَمِّی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ أَبِی الْقَاسِمِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَلِیٍّ عَنْ عِیسَی بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَنْ جَدِّهِ عَنْ عَلِیٍّ ع قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص لِلْمُؤَذِّنِ فِیمَا بَیْنَ الْأَذَانِ وَ الْإِقَامَهِ مِثْلُ أَجْرِ الشَّهِیدِ الْمُتَشَحِّطِ بِدَمِهِ فِی سَبِیلِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَی قَالَ فَقُلْتُ یَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ إِنَّهُمْ یَخْتَارُونَ الْأَذَانَ وَ الْإِقَامَهَ فَقَالَ کَلَّا إِنَّهُ یَأْتِی عَلَی النَّاسِ زَمَانٌ یَطْرَحُونَ الْأَذَانَ عَلَی ضُعَفَائِهِمْ فَتِلْکَ لُحُومٌ حَرَّمَهَا اللَّهُ عَلَی النَّارِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Ali b. Majluwayh who said it was narrated to me by my uncle Muhammad b. Abi al-Qasim from Muhammad b. Ali from Isa b. Abdillah from his father from his grandfather from Ali (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “No, a time will come when the responsibility of Azaan and Iqamah will be entrusted only to weak and helpless people. Hence the fire of Hell is made unlawful on their weak flesh.”

records.chapter: Reward of offering Salaat along with Azaan and Iqamah
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  For a person who recites Azaan and Iqamah angels would stand in rows behind him. Those rows would be so wide that  their  ends  would  be  unknown.  And  the  person  who offers  Salaat  after  saying  Iqamah;  an  angel  would  pray behind him.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره عَنْ سَعْدِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ سَلَمَهَ بْنِ الْخَطَّابِ عَنْ إِبْرَاهِیمَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ الثَّقَفِیِّ عَنْ مَیْمُونٍ عَنْ عَبْدِ الْمُطَّلِبِ بْنِ زِیَادٍ عَنْ أَبَانِ بْنِ تَغْلِبَ عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِی لَیْلَی عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ جَعْفَرٍ یَرْفَعُهُ قَالَ قَالَ عَلِیُّ بْنُ أَبِی طَالِبٍ ع مَنْ صَلَّی بِأَذَانٍ وَ إِقَامَهٍ صلاه [صَلَّی] خَلْفَهُ صَفٌّ مِنَ الْمَلَائِکَهِ لَا یُرَی طَرَفَاهُ- وَ مَنْ صَلَّی بِإِقَامَهٍ صلاه [صَلَّی] خَلْفَهُ مَلَکٌ.

records.chapter: Reward of offering Salaat along with Azaan and Iqamah
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 2.  Imam Ja’far Sadiq (a.s.) says, “One who offers Salaat after saying Azaan and Iqamah two rows of angels pray behind him. If a person offers Salaat after saying Iqamah only, one row of angels prays behind him.” The narrator asked, “How wide is that row?” Imam (a.s.) replied, “The shortest is from east to west and the longest from earth to heaven.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَلِیِّ بْنِ مَاجِیلَوَیْهِ ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی عَمِّی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ أَبِی الْقَاسِمِ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ سِنَانٍ عَنِ الْمُفَضَّلِ بْنِ عُمَرَ قَالَ قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع مَنْ صَلَّی بِأَذَانٍ وَ إِقَامَهٍ صلاه [صَلَّی] خَلْفَهُ صَفَّانِ مِنَ الْمَلَائِکَهِ وَ مَنْ صَلَّی بِإِقَامَهٍ بِغَیْرِ أَذَانٍ صَلَّی خَلْفَهُ صَفٌّ وَاحِدٌ قُلْتُ لَهُ وَ کَمْ مِقْدَارُ کُلِّ صَفٍّ فَقَالَ أَقَلُّهُ مَا بَیْنَ الْمَشْرِقِ وَ الْمَغْرِبِ وَ أَکْثَرُهُ مَا بَیْنَ السَّمَاءِ وَ الْأَرْضِ.
records.thaqalaynMatn: “One who offers Salaat after saying Azaan and Iqamah two rows of angels pray behind him. If a person offers Salaat after saying Iqamah only, one row of angels prays behind him.” The narrator asked, “How wide is that row?” Imam (a.s.) replied, “The shortest is from east to west and the longest from earth to heaven.”

records.chapter: Reward of reciting Surah Ikhlaas, Surah Qadr and Ayatal Kursi in every rakat of a recommended Salaat
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  Imam  Ja’far  Sadiq  (a.s.)  says,  “One  who  recites  Qul huwallaahu  ah’ad,  innaa  anzalnaahu  fee  lailatil  qadr  and ayatal kursi in a recommended prayer Allah would consider his deed best except for that person who has performed it more than him.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ یَحْیَی الْعَطَّارِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَحْمَدَ عَنْ سَهْلِ بْنِ الْحَسَنِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَلِیٍّ عَنْ عَلِیِّ بْنِ أَسْبَاطٍ عَنْ عَمِّهِ یَعْقُوبَ بْنِ سَالِمٍ عَنْ أَبِی الْحَسَنِ الْعَبْدِیِّ قَالَ قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع مَنْ قَرَأَ قُلْ هُوَ اللَّهُ أَحَدٌ وَ إِنَّا أَنْزَلْناهُ فِی لَیْلَهِ الْقَدْرِ وَ آیَهَ الْکُرْسِیِّ فِی کُلِّ رَکْعَهٍ مِنْ تَطَوُّعِهِ فَقَدْ فَتَحَ اللَّهُ لَهُ بِأَعْظَمِ أَعْمَالِ الْآدَمِیِّینَ إِلَّا مَنْ أَشْبَهَهُ فَزَادَ عَلَیْهِ.

records.chapter: Excellence and reward of Qunoot
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  It  is  narrated  by  Imam  Ja’far  Sadiq  (a.s.)  from  his predecessors  from  Abu  Zar  (r.a.)  that  the  Holy  Prophet (s.a.w.s.) said, “The longer the Qunoot of a person in this world, the longer would be his peace in Mauqif (stages) on the Day of Judgment.”
records.arabicText: 14- أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی أَحْمَدُ بْنُ إِدْرِیسَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَحْمَدَ عَنْ عَلِیِّ بْنِ إِسْمَاعِیلَ عَنْ صَفْوَانَ بْنِ یَحْیَی عَنْ أَبِی أَیُّوبَ عَنْ أَبِی بَصِیرٍ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ آبَائِهِ ع عَنْ أَبِی ذَرٍّ قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص أَطْوَلُکُمْ قُنُوتاً فِی دَارِ الدُّنْیَا أَطْوَلُکُمْ رَاحَهً یَوْمَ الْقِیَامَهِ فِی الْمَوْقِفِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1.  It  is  narrated  by  Imam  Ja’far  Sadiq  (a.s.)  from  his predecessors  from  Abu  Zar  (r.a.)  that  the  Holy  Prophet (s.a.w.s.) said, “
records.thaqalaynMatn: The longer the Qunoot of a person in this world, the longer would be his peace in Mauqif (stages) on the Day of Judgment.”

records.chapter: Reward of a complete Ruku
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Musa b. al-Mutawakil from Muhammad b. Yahya al-’Attar from Muhammad b. Ahmad from al-Sindi b. al-Rabee’ from Sa’eed b. Janah who said: “For one who completes his Ruku, fear won’t enter his grave.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُوسَی بْنِ الْمُتَوَکِّلِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ یَحْیَی الْعَطَّارِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَحْمَدَ عَنِ السِّنْدِیِّ بْنِ الرَّبِیعِ عَنْ سَعِیدِ بْنِ جَنَاحٍ قَالَ: کُنْتُ عِنْدَ أَبِی جَعْفَرٍ ع فِی مَنْزِلٍ بِالْمَدِینَهِ فَقَالَ مُبْتَدِئاً مَنْ أَتَمَّ رَکْعَهً لَمْ تَدْخُلْهُ وَحْشَهٌ فِی قَبْرِهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Musa b. al-Mutawakil from Muhammad b. Yahya al-’Attar from Muhammad b. Ahmad from al-Sindi b. al-Rabee’ from Sa’eed b. Janah who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “For one who completes his Ruku, fear won’t enter his grave.”

records.chapter: Reward of a Sajdah
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  Imam Ja’far Sadiq (a.s.) narrates from his predecessors that the Holy Prophet (s.a.w.s.) said, “One who prostrates once, one of his sins is forgiven and a position would be raised for him.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ الصَّفَّارُ عَنِ الْعَبَّاسِ بْنِ مَعْرُوفٍ عَنْ مُوسَی بْنِ الْقَاسِمِ عَنْ صَفْوَانَ بْنِ یَحْیَی عَنْ کُلَیْبٍ الصَّیْدَاوِیِّ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ آبَائِهِ ع قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص مَنْ سَجَدَ سَجْدَهً حُطَّ عَنْهُ بِهَا خَطِیئَهٌ وَ رُفِعَ لَهُ بِهَا دَرَجَهٌ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1.  Imam Ja’far Sadiq (a.s.) narrates from his predecessors that the Holy Prophet (s.a.w.s.) said,
records.thaqalaynMatn: “One who prostrates once, one of his sins is forgiven and a position would be raised for him.”

records.chapter: Reward of keeping the palms on the ground during Sajdah
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  Imam Ja’far Sadiq (a.s.) narrates from his predecessors that Amirul Momineen (a.s.) said, “When one of you performs the Sajdah you must place the palms of your hands on the ground,  perhaps  by  that  you  will  be  exempted  from  the chains on the Day of Qiyamat.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا عَلِیُّ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِیمَ عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَنِ النَّوْفَلِیِّ عَنِ السَّکُونِیِّ عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ آبَائِهِ ع قَالَ قَالَ أَمِیرُ الْمُؤْمِنِینَ ع إِذَا سَجَدَ أَحَدُکُمْ فَلْیُبَاشِرْ بِکَفَّیْهِ الْأَرْضَ لَعَلَّ اللَّهَ یَصْرِفُ عَنْهُ الْغُلَّ یَوْمَ الْقِیَامَهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1.  Imam Ja’far Sadiq (a.s.) narrates from his predecessors that Amirul Momineen (a.s.) said,
records.thaqalaynMatn: “When one of you performs the Sajdah you must place the palms of your hands on the ground,  perhaps  by  that  you  will  be  exempted  from  the chains on the Day of Qiyamat.”

records.chapter: Reward of prolonging a Sajdah
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  Imam  Ja’far  Sadiq  (a.s.)  said,  “One  who  performs  a prolonged  Sajdah  without  anyone  seeing  him  Shaitaan exclaims,   ‘Alas!   He   obeyed   while   I   disobeyed.   They prostrated and I refused.’”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره عَنْ سَعْدِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ الْحُسَیْنِ بْنِ سَعِیدٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِی عُمَیْرٍ عَنْ مُعَاوِیَهَ بْنِ عَمَّارٍ قَالَ سَمِعْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع وَ هُوَ یَقُولُ إِنَّ الْعَبْدَ إِذَا أَطَالَ السُّجُودَ حَیْثُ لَا یَرَاهُ أَحَدٌ قَالَ الشَّیْطَانُ وَا وَیْلَاهْ أَطَاعُوا وَ عَصَیْتُ وَ سَجَدُوا وَ أَبَیْتُ.

records.chapter: Reward of prolonging a Sajdah
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 2.  Imam Ja’far Sadiq (a.s.) says, “The state of a slave nearest to his Lord is the state of prostration.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره عَنْ سَعْدِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ الْحُسَیْنِ بْنِ سَعِیدٍ عَنْ فَضَالَهَ عَنِ الْعَلَاءِ بْنِ رَزِینٍ عَنْ زَیْدٍ الشَّحَّامِ قَالَ قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع أَقْرَبُ مَا یَکُونُ الْعَبْدُ إِلَی اللَّهِ وَ هُوَ سَاجِدٌ.

records.chapter: Reward of reciting Durood in the state of Ruku, Sajdah and Qiyam
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  Imam  Muhammad  Baqir  (a.s.)  says,  “One  who  recites: Allaahumma     s’alli     a’laa     muh’ammadinw     wa     aali muh’ammad in his Ruku, his Sajdah and his Qiyam, Allah writes for him a reward like that of Ruku, Sajdah or Qiyam.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَلِیِّ بْنِ مَاجِیلَوَیْهِ ره عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ یَحْیَی عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَحْمَدَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عِیسَی عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِی حَمْزَهَ عَنْ أَبِیهِ قَالَ قَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ ع مَنْ قَالَ فِی رُکُوعِهِ وَ سُجُودِهِ وَ قِیَامِهِ اللَّهُمَّ صَلِّ عَلَی مُحَمَّدٍ وَ آلِ مُحَمَّدٍ کَتَبَ اللَّهُ لَهُ ذَلِکَ بِمِثْلِ الرُّکُوعِ وَ السُّجُودِ وَ الْقِیَامِ.

records.chapter: Reward of Sajdah-e-Shukr
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  Imam  Ja’far  Sadiq  (a.s.)  says,  “One  who  prostrates  for thanking for the bounties apart from Salaat, Allah writes for him  ten  rewards,  erases  ten  of  his  sins  and  increase  his position by ten levels in Paradise.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ الصَّفَّارُ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ جَمِیلِ بْنِ صَالِحٍ عَنْ ذَرِیحٍ قَالَ قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع أَیُّمَا مُؤْمِنٍ سَجَدَ لِلَّهِ سَجْدَهً لِشُکْرِ نِعْمَهٍ مِنْ غَیْرِ صَلَاهٍ کَتَبَ اللَّهُ بِهَا عَشْرَ حَسَنَاتٍ وَ مَحَا عَنْهُ عَشْرَ سَیِّئَاتٍ وَ رَفَعَ لَهُ عَشْرَ دَرَجَاتٍ فِی الْجِنَانِ.

records.chapter: Reward of Salaat
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  Imam  Ja’far  Sadiq  (a.s.)  narrates  that  the  Holy  Prophet (s.a.w.s.) said, “Whenever the time of Salaat starts, an angel calls out to the people, ‘O people, stand up! Extinguish the fire, which you have lighted on your backs with the help of Salaat.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ یَحْیَی عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَحْمَدَ عَنْ مُوسَی بْنِ جَعْفَرٍ عَنْ عُبَیْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ وَاصِلِ بْنِ سُلَیْمَانَ وَ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ سِنَانٍ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص مَا مِنْ صَلَاهٍ یَحْضُرُ وَقْتُهَا إِلَّا نَادَی مَلَکٌ بَیْنَ یَدَیِ النَّاسِ أَیُّهَا النَّاسُ قُومُوا إِلَی نِیرَانِکُمُ الَّتِی أَوْقَدْتُمُوهَا عَلَی ظُهُورِکُمْ فَأَطْفِئُوهَا بِصَلَاتِکُمْ.

records.chapter: Reward of Salaat
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 2.  Imam  Ja’far  Sadiq  (a.s.)  says,  “O  Abdullah,  when  you offer an obligatory prayer, offer it on time. Else it is possible that  you  might  lose  the  opportunity  to  do  so.  Focus  your eyes on the place of your prostrations while offering Salaat. You pray carefully as if there is someone around you. Know that He is seeing you and you are not able to see Him.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ الصَّفَّارُ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْحُسَیْنِ بْنِ أَبِی الْخَطَّابِ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ عَبْدِ الْعَزِیزِ عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِی یَعْفُورٍ قَالَ قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع إِذَا صَلَّیْتَ صَلَاهً فَرِیضَهً فَصَلِّهَا لِوَقْتِهَا صَلَاهَ مُوَدِّعٍ یَخَافُ أَنْ لَا یَعُودَ إِلَیْهَا أَبَداً ثُمَّ اصْرِفْ بِبَصَرِکَ إِلَی مَوْضِعِ سُجُودِکَ فَلَوْ تَعْلَمُ مَنْ عَنْ یَمِینِکَ وَ شِمَالِکَ لَأَحْسَنْتَ صَلَاتَکَ وَ اعْلَمْ أَنَّکَ قُدَّامَ مَنْ یَرَاکَ وَ لَا تَرَاهُ.

records.chapter: Reward of Salaat
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 3. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Musa b. al-Mutawakil who said it was narrated to me by Ali b. al-Hussain al-Sa’dabadi from Ahmad b. Abi Abdillah al-Barqi from his father from Muhammad b. Abi ’Umayr from Jameel from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “There are three benefits of Salaat – whenever a person stands for Salaat, good deeds descend on him from all the corners of the heaven, angels surround that person from the place under his feet till the heaven and an angel calls out, ‘O worshipper! If you had known to Whom you are supplicating you would have never missed it.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُوسَی بْنِ الْمُتَوَکِّلِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی عَلِیُّ بْنُ الْحُسَیْنِ السَّعْدَآبَادِیُّ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ الْبَرْقِیِّ عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِی عُمَیْرٍ عَنْ جَمِیلٍ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: لِلْمُصَلِّی ثَلَاثُ خِصَالٍ إِذَا قَامَ فِی صَلَاتِهِ یَتَنَاثَرُ عَلَیْهِ الْبِرُّ مِنْ أَعْنَانِ السَّمَاءِ إِلَی مَفْرَقِ رَأْسِهِ وَ تَحُفُّ بِهِ الْمَلَائِکَهُ مِنْ قَدَمَیْهِ إِلَی أَعْنَانِ السَّمَاءِ وَ مَلَکٌ یُنَادِی أَیُّهَا الْمُصَلِّی لَوْ تَعْلَمُ مَنْ تُنَاجِی مَا انْفَتَلْتَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 3. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Musa b. al-Mutawakil who said it was narrated to me by Ali b. al-Hussain al-Sa’dabadi from Ahmad b. Abi Abdillah al-Barqi from his father from Muhammad b. Abi ’Umayr from Jameel from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “There are three benefits of Salaat – whenever a person stands for Salaat, good deeds descend on him from all the corners of the heaven, angels surround that person from the place under his feet till the heaven and an angel calls out, ‘O worshipper! If you had known to Whom you are supplicating you would have never missed it.”

records.chapter: Reward of offering Morning Salaat in its prime time
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan from Muhammad b. al-Hassan al-Saffar from al-Hassan b. Musa al-Khashab from Abdillah b. Jabalah from Ghiyath b. Kalub from Ishaq b. Ammar who said: I asked Abi Abdillah (a.s.): "Oh Aba Abdillah, inform me about the best times during the Fajr prayer." He replied: "It is at the time of the dawn. Verily, Allah, the Most High, says, 'Indeed, the recitation of the Qur'an at dawn is ever witnessed.' (17:78). Meaning, it is witnessed by the angels of the day and the angels of the night. So when the servant prays Salaat al-Subh at the start of dawn, its reward would be noted down twice, noted by the angels of the night and the angels of the day.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْحَسَنِ الصَّفَّارِ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ مُوسَی الْخَشَّابِ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ جَبَلَهَ عَنْ غِیَاثِ بْنِ کَلُّوبٍ عَنْ إِسْحَاقَ بْنِ عَمَّارٍ قَالَ: قُلْتُ لِأَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع یَا أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ أَخْبِرْنِی عَنْ أَفْضَلِ الْمَوَاقِیتِ فِی صَلَاهِ الْفَجْرِ قَالَ مَعَ طُلُوعِ الْفَجْرِ إِنَّ اللَّهَ تَعَالَی یَقُولُ- إِنَّ قُرْآنَ الْفَجْرِ کانَ مَشْهُوداً یَعْنِی صَلَاهَ الْفَجْرِ تَشْهَدُهَا مَلَائِکَهُ النَّهَارِ وَ مَلَائِکَهُ اللَّیْلِ فَإِذَا صَلَّی الْعَبْدُ صَلَاهَ الصُّبْحِ مَعَ طُلُوعِ الْفَجْرِ أُثْبِتَتْ لَهُ مَرَّتَیْنِ تُثْبِتُهَا مَلَائِکَهُ اللَّیْلِ وَ مَلَائِکَهُ النَّهَارِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan from Muhammad b. al-Hassan al-Saffar from al-Hassan b. Musa al-Khashab from Abdillah b. Jabalah from Ghiyath b. Kalub from Ishaq b. Ammar who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: I asked Abi Abdillah (a.s.): "Oh Aba Abdillah, inform me about the best times during the Fajr prayer." He replied: "It is at the time of the dawn. Verily, Allah, the Most High, says, 'Indeed, the recitation of the Qur'an at dawn is ever witnessed.' (17:78). Meaning, it is witnessed by the angels of the day and the angels of the night. So when the servant prays Salaat al-Subh at the start of dawn, its reward would be noted down twice, noted by the angels of the night and the angels of the day.”

records.chapter: Excellence of prime time over the last moments
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  Imam  Ja’far  Sadiq (a.s.) says, “The  excellence of prime time over the last moments for a believer is more than his children and his wealth.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُوسَی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ الْعَبَّاسِ بْنِ مَعْرُوفٍ عَنْ بَکْرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ الْأَزْدِیِّ قَالَ قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع فَضْلُ الْوَقْتِ الْأَوَّلِ عَلَی الْأَخِیرِ [خَیْرٌ] لِلْمُؤْمِنِ [مِنْ] وُلْدِهِ وَ مَالِهِ

records.chapter: Excellence of prime time over the last moments
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 2.  Imam  Ja’far  Sadiq (a.s.) says, “The  excellence of prime time  over  the  last  moments  is  like  the  excellence  of Hereafter over this world.”
records.arabicText: وَ فِی حَدِیثٍ آخَرَ قَالَ الصَّادِقُ ع فَضْلُ الْوَقْتِ الْأَوَّلِ عَلَی الْأَخِیرِ کَفَضْلِ الْآخِرَهِ عَلَی الدُّنْیَا.

records.chapter: Reward of offering obligatory prayers during prime time
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. Abi (rah) from Sa’d b. Abdillah from Ahmad b. Muhammad from al-Hassan b. Mahbub from Sa’d b. Abi Khalaf from Abi al-Hassan Musa (a.s.) who said: “If an obligatory prayer is offered in prime time fulfilling all the conditions a sweet smell would come out from it just like the sweet smell that comes out when a branch of tree is broken. Hence it is obligatory on you to offer Salaat at its prime time.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره عَنْ سَعْدِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ سَعْدِ بْنِ أَبِی خَلَفٍ عَنْ أَبِی الْحَسَنِ مُوسَی ع قَالَ: الصَّلَوَاتُ الْمَفْرُوضَاتُ فِی أَوَّلِ وَقْتِهَا إِذَا أُقِیمَ حُدُودُهَا أَطْیَبُ رِیحاً مِنْ قَضِیبِ الْآسِ حِینَ یُؤْخَذُ مِنْ شَجَرِهِ فِی طِیبِهِ وَ رِیحِهِ وَ طَرَاوَتِهِ فَعَلَیْکُمْ بِالْوَقْتِ الْأَوَّلِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. Abi (rah) from Sa’d b. Abdillah from Ahmad b. Muhammad from al-Hassan b. Mahbub from Sa’d b. Abi Khalaf from Abi al-Hassan Musa (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “If an obligatory prayer is offered in prime time fulfilling all the conditions a sweet smell would come out from it just like the sweet smell that comes out when a branch of tree is broken. Hence it is obligatory on you to offer Salaat at its prime time.”
